title: Psychotria camptopus
taxonomy: Psychotria camptopus is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Psychotria is a group of evergreen shrubs and small trees native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Psychotria camptopus is specifically a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species is classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other species within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Psychotria camptopus, also known as "Bado", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Psychotria camptopus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in herbal supplements and capsules.
